text: While multi-query attention (MQA) gets the decoder inference speedup from just using a single key-value head, it typically comes with a decrease in model performance. In addition, retraining a model to trade off performance for faster inference is generally not an option. This work contains two main contributions. We show a very fast way (requiring only 5% of the original pre-training compute) to “uptrain” a multilo-head language model checkpoint to one that supports MQA. We also introduce a new attention mechanism — grouped-query attention (GQA) — with an intermediate number of key-value heads. While GQA’s performance is almost identical to the original multilo-head attention, it retains the inference speed of the much more efficient MQA.
explanation: The text reads like a concise, domain-specific abstract with coherent argument and field terminology, which is common in human-authored papers. However, the precise claims and phrasing could also be produced by a capable AI, so there is notable ambiguity.
